description: RadioMaster Pocket (EdgeTX joystick/HID firmware mode, USB VID 0x1209 / PID 0x4F54). Verified empirically with tools/hid-probe against the physical unit: the HID report descriptor declares 24 buttons (3 packed bytes) followed by 8 Generic Desktop axes (X, Y, Z, Rx, Ry, Rz, Slider, Dial), each a 16-bit field carrying an 11-bit logical range 0..2047, for a 19-byte input report. Axis order is assumed to follow EdgeTX's default AETR channel order (source_index 0=roll/aileron, 1=pitch/elevator, 2=throttle, 3=yaw/rudder, 4..7=aux1..aux4); this was not cross-checked against physical stick movement (sticks were unattended/idle during capture), only against the resting-value pattern. Resting raw values observed were [1024, 1024, 0, 1024, 0, 0, 0, 0]: source_index 0, 1, 3 sit at 1024 (the midpoint of 0..2047), consistent with self-centering roll/pitch/yaw sticks; source_index 2 (throttle) sits at 0, consistent with a non-centering throttle resting at idle; source_index 4..7 (aux) sit at 0 with no observed switch activity. Calibration below uses the full declared logical range on the positive side (max=2047) with center taken from the observed resting value per axis; roll/pitch/yaw use min=0 since their resting center (1024) sits strictly between the declared 0..2047 bounds. Throttle and aux1..aux4 rest at raw 0 with no negative deflection ever produced by the hardware: the true logical range is 0..2047 with idle at the 0 boundary, which is not a center in the schema's sense. Because `parse_profile_str` rejects `min == center` as a degenerate calibration, min is set to a negligible epsilon (-0.001, three orders of magnitude below one raw HID count) below center=0 purely to satisfy that strict ordering; it does not assert the hardware can produce a negative value, and the negative half of the normalized range for these axes is never reachable in practice. Deadzone and expo are conservative defaults pending a live-stick recalibration pass.
